summary:
Iron deficiency anemia presents with low serum ferritin, elevated total iron-binding capacity (TIBC), and reduced transferrin saturation. These laboratory findings reflect depleted iron stores and impaired iron utilization, forming the cornerstone of diagnosis. 

Note: This summary focuses solely on clinically significant aspects related to detecting iron deficiency anemia based strictly upon the agent’s analysis. It does not include treatment details unless explicitly tied to diagnostics.
